```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)
library(tm)
library(ggrepel) 
```
# STEP 1 — LOAD DATASET

```{r}
# Load dataset from CSV file
movies <- read.csv(
  "D:/Desktop/movie_recommender/data/movies_metadata.csv",
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Display first few rows in table format
head(movies,5)
```
# STEP 2 — DATASET INFORMATION

```{r}
# Display structure — column names, types, sample values
cat("DATASET STRUCTURE:\n")
str(movies)
```
```{r}
# Summary statistics for all columns
cat("DATASET SUMMARY:\n")
summary(movies)
```
```{r}
# Dataset dimensions
cat("Rows    :", nrow(movies), "\n")
cat("Columns :", ncol(movies), "\n")
cat("Columns :", paste(colnames(movies), collapse = ", "), "\n")
```

# STEP 3 — MISSING VALUE ANALYSIS

```{r}
missing_before <- colSums(is.na(movies))
cat("Missing values per column (before cleaning):\n")
print(missing_before)

# Visualize missing values
missing_df <- data.frame(
  Column  = names(missing_before),
  Missing = as.numeric(missing_before)
) %>% arrange(desc(Missing)) %>% filter(Missing > 0)

if (nrow(missing_df) > 0) {
  ggplot(missing_df, aes(x = reorder(Column, Missing),
                          y = Missing, fill = Missing)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.7) +
    scale_fill_gradient(low = "#fee8c8", high = "#d7301f") +
    coord_flip() +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(legend.position = "none",
          plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14)) +
    labs(title = "Missing Values Before Cleaning",
         x = NULL, y = "Missing Count")
}
ggsave("results/figures/missing_values.png", width = 8, height = 5)
```

# STEP 4 — DATA TYPE CONVERSION

```{r}
# Identify non-numeric popularity values
cat("NON-NUMERIC POPULARITY VALUES:\n")
print(movies[is.na(suppressWarnings(as.numeric(movies$popularity))),
             c("title", "popularity")])
```

```{r}
# Filter essential columns and convert to correct types
movies_temp <- movies %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(title)    & nchar(trimws(title))    > 0,
    !is.na(genres)   & nchar(trimws(genres))   > 0,
    !is.na(overview) & nchar(trimws(overview)) > 10
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    popularity   = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(popularity)),
    vote_average = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(vote_average)),
    vote_count   = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(vote_count))
  )

cat("ROWS AFTER TYPE CONVERSION:", nrow(movies_temp), "\n")
```
```{r}
# Check missing values after conversion
cat("MISSING VALUES AFTER TYPE CONVERSION:\n")
print(colSums(is.na(movies_temp)))
```
# STEP 5 — DATA CLEANING

```{r}
# Remove rows with invalid numeric values
movies_clean <- movies_temp %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(popularity)   & popularity   >= 0,
    !is.na(vote_average) & vote_average >= 0 & vote_average <= 10,
    !is.na(vote_count)   & vote_count   >= 0
  )

cat("ROWS AFTER REMOVING INVALID VALUES:", nrow(movies_clean), "\n")
cat("ROWS REMOVED                      :",
    nrow(movies_temp) - nrow(movies_clean), "\n")
```

```{r}
# Remove duplicate titles — keep highest vote_count
movies_clean <- movies_clean %>%
  arrange(desc(vote_count)) %>%
  distinct(title, .keep_all = TRUE)

cat("ROWS AFTER DEDUPLICATION:", nrow(movies_clean), "\n")
```

```{r}
# Confirm no missing values remain
cat("MISSING VALUES AFTER FULL CLEANING:\n")
print(colSums(is.na(movies_clean)))
```
```{r}
head(movies_clean[, 1:6])
```
# STEP 6 — FEATURE ENGINEERING

```{r}
# Select required columns

movies_final <- movies_clean %>%
  select(title, genres, overview, vote_average, vote_count, popularity, original_language)

# Combine genres and overview for TF-IDF content feature
movies_final$content <- paste(
  movies_final$genres,
  movies_final$overview
)

# Normalize numerical features using z-score scaling
movies_final$popularity_scaled <- as.numeric(scale(movies_final$popularity))
movies_final$rating_scaled     <- as.numeric(scale(movies_final$vote_average))
movies_final$votes_scaled      <- as.numeric(scale(log1p(movies_final$vote_count)))

# Composite score for hybrid recommendation ranking
movies_final$final_score <-
  movies_final$popularity_scaled +
  movies_final$rating_scaled     +
  movies_final$votes_scaled
cat("FEATURE ENGINEERING COMPLETED\n\n")
```
```{r}
cat("TOP 10 MOVIES BY FINAL SCORE:\n")

top_movies <- movies_final %>%
  arrange(desc(final_score)) %>%
  select(title, popularity, vote_average, vote_count, final_score) %>%
  head(10)

print(top_movies)

ggplot(top_movies, aes(x = reorder(title, final_score), y = final_score, fill = final_score)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "lightblue", high = "darkblue") +
  labs(
    title = "Top 10 Movies by Final Score",
    x = "Movie Title",
    y = "Final Score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
ggsave("results/figures/top10_final_score.png", width = 8, height = 5)
```
# STEP 7 — EXPLORATORY DATA ANALYSIS

```{r}
# Distribution of vote averages
ggplot(movies_final, aes(x = vote_average)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40, fill = "#4292c6",
                 color = "white", alpha = 0.85) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14)) +
  labs(title    = "Distribution of Movie Ratings",
       subtitle = "How ratings are distributed across all movies",
       x = "Vote Average", y = "Count")
ggsave("results/figures/rating_distribution.png", width = 8, height = 5)
```

```{r}
# Relationship between popularity and rating
ggplot(
  movies_final %>%
    filter(
      popularity > 0,
      popularity < quantile(popularity, 0.99)
    ),
  aes(x = vote_average, y = popularity)
) +
  geom_point(aes(color = vote_average, size = vote_count), alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE,
              color = "#d62728", linewidth = 1) +
  scale_color_gradientn(colors = c("#fee8c8", "#e34a33", "#7f0000")) +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(0.5, 6)) +
  scale_y_log10(labels = scales::comma) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14)) +
  labs(
    title = "Popularity vs Vote Average",
    subtitle = "Bubble size = Vote Count | Y-axis log scale",
    x = "Vote Average",
    y = "Popularity (log scale)",
    color = "Rating",
    size = "Votes"
  )
ggsave("results/figures/popularity_vs_rating.png", width = 8, height = 5)
```

```{r}
# Genre distribution — extract genre names and plot
get_genre_list <- function(genre_str) {
  matches <- regmatches(genre_str,
               gregexpr("'name':\\s*'([^']+)'", genre_str))[[1]]
  tolower(gsub("'name':\\s*'([^']+)'", "\\1", matches))
}

all_genres <- unlist(lapply(movies_final$genres, get_genre_list))
genre_freq <- sort(table(all_genres), decreasing = TRUE)
genre_df   <- data.frame(
  Genre = names(genre_freq),
  Count = as.numeric(genre_freq)
) %>% filter(nchar(Genre) > 0) %>% head(15)

ggplot(genre_df, aes(x = reorder(Genre, Count),
                      y = Count, fill = Count)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.72) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Count), hjust = -0.2,
            size = 3.2, color = "#555") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colors = c("#fcbba1", "#cb181d", "#67000d")) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1))) +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank()) +
  labs(title    = "Top 15 Movie Genres",
       subtitle = "Number of movies per genre",
       x = NULL, y = "Count")
ggsave("results/figures/top15_genres.png", width = 8, height = 5)
```

```{r}
# Remove invalid values
movies_filtered <- movies_final %>%
  filter(popularity > 0)

# IQR calculation
Q1 <- quantile(movies_filtered$popularity, 0.25)
Q3 <- quantile(movies_filtered$popularity, 0.75)
IQR_val <- Q3 - Q1
upper_bound <- Q3 + 1.5 * IQR_val

# Select top outliers to label (avoid clutter)
top_outliers <- movies_filtered %>%
  filter(popularity > upper_bound) %>%
  arrange(desc(popularity)) %>%
  head(5)

# Plot
ggplot(movies_filtered, aes(x = "", y = popularity)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "#67000d", outlier.color = "#cb181d") +
  
  # Label top outlier movies
  geom_text_repel(
    data = top_outliers,
    aes(label = title),
    size = 4,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  
  scale_y_log10() +
  
  labs(
    title = "Boxplot of Movie Popularity with Outlier Titles",
    x = "",
    y = "Popularity (log scale)"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal()
ggsave("results/figures/popularity_boxplot.png", width = 8, height = 5)
```


# STEP 8 — TEXT PREPROCESSING (TF-IDF)

```{r}
# Build corpus from content column
cat("BUILDING TF-IDF MATRIX...\n")
corpus <- Corpus(VectorSource(movies_final$content))

# Text preprocessing pipeline
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, content_transformer(tolower))
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, content_transformer(function(x)
  gsub("[^a-z ]", " ", x)))
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, content_transformer(function(x)
  removeWords(x, c(stopwords("english"),
                   "film", "movie", "story", "life", "one",
                   "man", "woman", "find", "must", "will"))))
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, content_transformer(stripWhitespace))

# Build TF-IDF Document Term Matrix
dtm <- DocumentTermMatrix(
  corpus,
  control = list(
    weighting   = weightTfIdf,
    bounds      = list(global = c(5, Inf)),
    wordLengths = c(4, 25)
  )
)

cat("TF-IDF MATRIX DIMENSIONS:\n")
cat("Documents :", nrow(dtm), "\n")
cat("Terms     :", ncol(dtm), "\n")
```

```{r}
# Top 20 most significant TF-IDF terms
tfidf_mat   <- as.matrix(dtm)
term_scores <- sort(colSums(tfidf_mat), decreasing = TRUE)[1:20]
term_df <- data.frame(
  Term  = factor(names(term_scores),
                 levels = names(term_scores)[order(term_scores)]),
  Score = as.numeric(term_scores)
)

ggplot(term_df, aes(x = Term, y = Score, fill = Score)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.72) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(Score, 1)),
            hjust = -0.15, size = 3, color = "#888") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colors = c("#c6dbef", "#2171b5", "#08306b")) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.12))) +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank()) +
  labs(title    = "Top 20 Terms by TF-IDF Score",
       subtitle = "Most significant content words used in recommendation",
       x = NULL, y = "TF-IDF Score")
ggsave("results/figures/tfidf_top20_terms.png", width = 8, height = 5)
```

# STEP 9 — FINAL DATASET SUMMARY

```{r}
# Final dataset overview
cat(" FINAL DATASET SUMMARY \n\n")
cat("Total Movies :", nrow(movies_final), "\n")
cat("Columns      :", paste(colnames(movies_final), collapse = ", "), "\n\n")

cat("Missing Values in Final Dataset:\n")
print(colSums(is.na(movies_final)))
```


```{r}
cat("FINAL PREPROCESSED DATASET:\n")

final_preview <- movies_final %>%
  select(title, vote_average, vote_count, popularity,original_language, final_score) %>%
  head(10)

print(final_preview)
```
# STEP 10 — SAVE FINAL DATASET

```{r}
# Save cleaned and engineered dataset for model building
write.csv(
  movies_final,
  "D:/Desktop/movie_recommender/data/movies_final.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)

cat("Dataset saved successfully.\n")
cat("Final rows:", nrow(movies_final), "\n")
cat("Final cols:", ncol(movies_final), "\n\n")

cat("FINAL DATA READY FOR MODEL BUILDING\n")
```
